sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Larry, has been experiencing chronic back pain for a couple of years, initially occurring after moving furniture.
The pain is described as sharp stabbing and radiates down to his right leg, specifically to his right big toe.
He also experiences numbness and tingling in his right leg, but no loss of sensation in the genital or rectal area, and no weakness.
He has tried physical therapy, strong pain medications, and has been considered for surgery, but would like to avoid it.
His daily activities are limited due to back pain, which has led to weight gain.
He used to enjoy playing golf but has been unable to participate due to his back pain.
Past medical history: The patient denies any family members with spine conditions and does not smoke, having quit 20 years ago.
He has not mentioned any other past medical conditions or relevant social history.
Review of systems: The patient reports numbness and tingling in his right leg, but denies any loss of sensation, weakness, loss of control of bladder or bowels, or other symptoms.
Current medications: Not mentioned.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient has no overlying erythema or ecchymosis on his back.
There is midline tenderness at L4-L5 disk space with right-sided lumbar paravertebral tenderness.
The patient experiences pain with lumbar flexion and a positive straight leg test.
The neurological examination reveals decreased sensation to light touch at the right lateral thigh at L4 dermatome.
Patellar and Achilles reflexes are symmetrical.
Test Results: The patient's MRI shows disk herniation with nerve impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are consistent with an L4-L5 herniated disk with radiculopathy.
Problem list: Herniated disk at L4-L5; Radiculopathy; Chronic back pain.
Plan:
I recommended a spinal injection with a corticosteroid, which will be administered under fluoroscopy to help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
I will monitor the patient's response to the injection and schedule follow-up appointments as needed.
I explained to the patient that the procedure is relatively quick and common, and he should be able to return to normal activities within an hour.
Follow-up: The patient will return for follow-up after the procedure to assess his response and determine if further injections are necessary.
